mnemonics: Baysian Rule: Believe It If You See It, Ignore It If You Don't
keyPoints: The Bayes' theorem is used for classification by calculating the probability of an event based on prior knowledge and evidence.
Posterior probability is calculated using the formula P(A|B) = P(B|A) * P(A) / P(B)
In Bayesian classification, the model is trained using the Bayesian classifier, which uses Bayes theorem to calculate the probability of an instance belonging to a class.
